matter.title: Merle A Thibodeau v. Nadia P. Loe (leap-day collision)
matter.summary: Merle A Thibodeau, a leap-day-born 98-year-old, was struck in the crosswalk at Main Street and Water Street, Bangor at 23:58 on 2024-02-29; suit filed 2026-01-01, limitations running to 2030-02-28.

fact_pattern.summary: Merle A Thibodeau, a leap-day-born 98-year-old, was struck in the crosswalk at Main Street and Water Street, Bangor at 23:58 on 2024-02-29; suit filed 2026-01-01, limitations running to 2030-02-28.
fact_pattern.narrative:
On 2024-02-29 — a leap day — at 23:58, Merle A Thibodeau, then 98 years old and himself born on a February 29, was struck by a vehicle driven by Nadia P. Loe in the crosswalk at Main Street and Water Street, Bangor. He was treated at the emergency department the same date, 2024-02-29.

The complaint is filed on 2026-01-01. Under 14 M.R.S. § 752 (six years), the limitations period runs from the leap-day accident to 2030-02-28, because 2030 has no February 29.

Counsel Priya C. Roe of Hawthorne Legal Group demands $210314 for the fracture injuries, ambulance, and rehabilitation.
undisputed_facts: The collision occurred on 2024-02-29 at about 23:58 in the crosswalk at Main Street and Water Street, Bangor.
disputed_facts: Comparative fault in a dark crosswalk two minutes before midnight.
The extent of aggravation of pre-existing conditions in a 98-year-old plaintiff.

timeline.event: Collision in the Main Street crosswalk at 23:58.
Emergency-department treatment at St. Joseph Hospital (same calendar day).
Statute of limitations expires (14 M.R.S. § 752 (six years)).

exchanges.question: When exactly did this happen?
exchanges.answer: February 29, 2024 — leap day, my birthday — at two minutes to midnight, in the crosswalk at Main Street and Water Street, Bangor.

exchanges.question: How were you hurt?
exchanges.answer: A fractured hip and wrist; I was treated that same night and did months of rehabilitation.

exchanges.question: Why file now?
exchanges.answer: Counsel said we're well inside the six-year period, which runs to 2030-02-28.

sub_questions: Was the plaintiff's late-night crossing a contributing cause?

issues.issue: Limitations computation from a leap-day accrual
sub_questions: Does the period expire 2030-02-28 or March 1, 2030?

evidence.title: Police report 24-000001
evidence.description: Scene report dated the day after the leap-day collision.

amounts.label: General damages demand
amounts.basis: Pain, suffering, and loss of enjoyment.

facts.accident_location: the crosswalk at Main Street and Water Street, Bangor
